channel.title: Comments for page "Site News Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Site News Hub"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ?Why I can't see the AV??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that Nature vs Naturalization was a collaboration between myself and DarkStuff

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does anyone else notice there are two of the same FAQ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And again, another good Site News !

Seriously, I totally like this because we can discover great things, new SCP, new Tales, new Misc and great artists ! And that's a proof that we lived a real Golden Age. Boy, I'm sooooooooo glad and proud to be a part of the community.

So, great job, Decibelles, and continue to still kicking asses with another f***ing great creations !

item.title: News Hub Update
item.content:encoded: When will the "News Hub" get an update?

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Dr David Brajkovic, this really isn't the place to be asking questions about your own SCP projects. Please use the Help Forums in the listing here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum:start

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you put a sound file in the scp article and if yes how?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In addition to that, if an author is unsatisfied by a blurb in that month's news, they are free to directly edit the page themselves and put their own blurb in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every article in the site news has little summaries and taglines about them, called blurbs, to catch people's eyes.

Normally the site news editors create blurbs for all articles and then let authors change them after posting if they're unsatisfied with the created blurb. The third question basically says that any author can skip the process by directly sending the editors blurbs for articles that they have written that are in that months news.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff from the KO wiki. Planning to adopt this format into our wiki. Could you explain the purpose of the third question in more detail?

item.title: Re: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
Just to clarify how I find art to post, I use five sources, in the following order of how much content I'm able to get from them:

  • pixiv
  • tumblr
  • deviantArt
  • Twitter
  • Reddit

There's not many restrictions, though I have a few:

  1. Art can't be NSFW. Light gore/violence is fine. Stylized gore/heavy violence is allowed if part of a pixiv photoset and not the cover image of said photoset).
  2. The artist doesn't have NSFW art mixed with their SFW art. I'm fine with them having separate NSFW art on another blog/account, but if they have it mixed with their main art, no-go.
  3. In the case of pixiv, I don't include an artist if they have NSFW art in the first page of their bookmarks (because I don't have the time to search through every single artist's bookmark pages).

This is just to ensure I don't link anything of this nature to the under-18 crowd who uses this site and also not link to content that breaks Wikidot's Terms of Services. Better safe than sorry.

item.title: June 2016?
item.content:encoded:
Any ideas on when the June post will be up?

If you need people to help you out to get it done faster, PM me, I'm willing to help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: in all actuality, she probably could. Looking forward to the April addition

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops I ment February! ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Decibelle will gaze into her crystal ball and give us information on the upcoming March. Her vision is true.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mean, February? March has only begun… unless Decibelles can see into the future?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't wait for the march one! I love these!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: February's edition will be delayed until the final day of posting, in order to ensure all contest entries are displayed on the same day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww yiss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you Quikngruvn for adding it to the sidebar!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this in its entirety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree—this is exactly the kind of thing that should be easily found on the left-hand menu. New visitors/members would find it extremely helpful, and it does a great job of showing just how alive the site is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ayyy, news hub! Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I get in touch with an admin, I wanna see if I can do even better, and get a sidebar/dropdown menu link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can this be linked from the main page, so that it can read in later months and be easily accessible?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Deci, I really admire how much time you devote to the wiki and the fandom. We all really respect and appreciate your effort in creating things like this. We love you, and keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome! It's so cool that you're taking the time to do this. Here's to another year of SCP fun!

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who helped me out with this the past few months, to staff for supporting me, and to the community and fandom at large for making both this site wonderful, and for being relatively stable and peaceful and producing wonderful content. This is quite the project, and I'm still ironing out the final wrinkles with how to update it monthly.

Special huge thanks to the Russian site for doing this in the first place, and having it be such a cool concept that I was inspired to want to do it as well, and find a way to make it my own. You can find their version here.

+ How I pick what articles appear on here.
- How I pick what articles appear on here.

The only firm criteria is that an article must not be in the negatives at all. Beyond that, whoever is writing the new installment will pick based on discretion. One example: if an article appears to be rapidly sinking for various reasons by the time a new installment is written (and we've had cases of this happening before), then it will not be shown. If an article seems to be dipping in and out of the negatives, it may not be shown as well.

- How I pick what articles appear on here.

This is honestly my favorite thing to work on for the site. I hope to continue it every month (or at least entrust it in the care of Community Outreach if emergencies come up :P).

Love you all! ❤
